### Initialize Netlify Project via CLI

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/static-deploy.md

This command initializes a new Netlify site from the current project directory. It guides the user through the setup process, linking the local project to a Netlify site.

```bash
netlify init
```

--------------------------------

### Install Project Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/packages/create-vite/template-qwik/README.md

Command to install all project dependencies using npm, pnpm, or yarn package managers. This must be run before starting development or building the application.

```bash
npm install
```

--------------------------------

### preview

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-javascript.md

Starts a Vite preview server instance with optional inline configuration.

```APIDOC
## preview

### Description
Starts a Vite preview server instance with optional inline configuration.

### Signature
```ts
async function preview(inlineConfig?: InlineConfig): Promise<PreviewServer>
```

### Parameters
- **inlineConfig** (InlineConfig) - Optional - Configuration options for the preview server.

### Return Value
(Promise<PreviewServer>) - A promise that resolves to a PreviewServer instance.

### Example Usage
```ts
import { preview } from 'vite'

const previewServer = await preview({
  // any valid user config options, plus `mode` and `configFile`
  preview: {
    port: 8080,
    open: true,
  },
})

previewServer.printUrls()
previewServer.bindCLIShortcuts({ print: true })
```
```

--------------------------------

### Scaffold a project using a community template with tiged

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/packages/create-vite/README.md

Utilize `tiged` to scaffold a project from a community-maintained template, then navigate into the new directory, install dependencies, and start the development server.

```bash
npx tiged user/project my-project
cd my-project

npm install
npm run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies with npm, pnpm, or yarn

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/packages/create-vite/template-solid-ts/README.md

Install project dependencies using one of the supported package managers. This command downloads all required packages specified in package.json and prepares the project for development.

```bash
npm install
```

--------------------------------

### Scaffold a project from a GitHub repository using tiged

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Use `tiged` to clone a project template from GitHub, then install dependencies and start the development server.

```bash
npx tiged user/project my-project
cd my-project

npm install
npm run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Project Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/packages/create-vite/template-qwik-ts/README.md

Standard command to install project dependencies using npm, pnpm, or yarn. This step is required before running the development server or building the project for production.

```bash
$ npm install # or pnpm install or yarn install
```

--------------------------------

### Configure package.json for Vite Library Distribution

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/build.md

These `package.json` examples demonstrate the recommended setup for distributing a Vite-built library. They define the package name, type, included files, and crucially, the `exports` field to specify entry points for different module systems (ESM via `import` and CommonJS via `require`), supporting both single and multiple library entries.

```json
{
  "name": "my-lib",
  "type": "module",
  "files": ["dist"],
  "main": "./dist/my-lib.umd.cjs",
  "module": "./dist/my-lib.js",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "import": "./dist/my-lib.js",
      "require": "./dist/my-lib.umd.cjs"
    }
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "name": "my-lib",
  "type": "module",
  "files": ["dist"],
  "main": "./dist/my-lib.cjs",
  "module": "./dist/my-lib.js",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "import": "./dist/my-lib.js",
      "require": "./dist/my-lib.cjs"
    },
    "./secondary": {
      "import": "./dist/secondary.js",
      "require": "./dist/secondary.cjs"
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Run the Vite development server

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Start the Vite development server to serve your application. This command will typically serve `index.html` on `http://localhost:5173`.

```bash
npx vite
```

```bash
yarn vite
```

```bash
pnpm vite
```

```bash
bunx vite
```

```bash
deno run -A npm:vite
```

--------------------------------

### Start Vite dev server

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/cli.md

Basic usage pattern for starting the Vite development server in the current directory or a specified root directory.

```bash
vite [root]
```

--------------------------------

### Install Babel Plugin for Decorators

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/migration.md

Package installation commands for @rolldown/plugin-babel and @babel/plugin-proposal-decorators across different package managers.

```bash
$ npm install -D @rolldown/plugin-babel @babel/plugin-proposal-decorators
```

```bash
$ yarn add -D @rolldown/plugin-babel @babel/plugin-proposal-decorators
```

```bash
$ pnpm add -D @rolldown/plugin-babel @babel/plugin-proposal-decorators
```

```bash
$ bun add -D @rolldown/plugin-babel @babel/plugin-proposal-decorators
```

```bash
$ deno add -D npm:@rolldown/plugin-babel npm:@babel/plugin-proposal-decorators
```

--------------------------------

### Preview Server Initialization with Vite

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-javascript.md

Initializes a Vite preview server with custom port and auto-opening the browser. This snippet demonstrates how to programmatically start and manage a preview server.

```typescript
import { preview } from 'vite'

const previewServer = await preview({
  // any valid user config options, plus `mode` and `configFile`
  preview: {
    port: 8080,
    open: true,
  },
})

previewServer.printUrls()
previewServer.bindCLIShortcuts({ print: true })
```

--------------------------------

### Configure CSS Preprocessor Options

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/shared-options.md

Example showing how to pass specific options to Less, Stylus, and SCSS preprocessors using "css.preprocessorOptions" in Vite's configuration.

```js
export default defineConfig({
  css: {
    preprocessorOptions: {
      less: {
        math: 'parens-division',
      },
      styl: {
        define: {
          $specialColor: new stylus.nodes.RGBA(51, 197, 255, 1),
        },
      },
      scss: {
        importers: [
          // ...
        ],
      },
    },
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### listen

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-javascript.md

Starts the Vite development server, making it listen on a specified port.

```APIDOC
## listen

### Description
Starts the Vite development server, making it listen on a specified port.

### Signature
```typescript
listen(port?: number, isRestart?: boolean): Promise<ViteDevServer>
```

### Parameters
- **port** (number) - Optional - The port number for the server to listen on.
- **isRestart** (boolean) - Optional - Indicates if the server is being restarted.

### Returns
`Promise<ViteDevServer>` - A promise that resolves to the `ViteDevServer` instance once it starts listening.
```

--------------------------------

### Standard Vite HTML entry point structure

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/features.md

Example of an HTML file where assets and module scripts are automatically processed by Vite.

```html
<!doctype html>
<html>
  <head>
    <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" />
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="/src/styles.css" />
  </head>
  <body>
    <img src="/src/images/logo.svg" alt="logo" />
    <script type="module" src="/src/main.js"></script>
  </body>
</html>
```

--------------------------------

### Install Netlify CLI

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/static-deploy.md

This command installs the Netlify Command Line Interface globally using npm. The Netlify CLI allows users to interact with Netlify services, manage sites, and deploy projects directly from their terminal.

```bash
npm install -g netlify-cli
```

--------------------------------

### Install esbuild for CSS Minification

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/build-options.md

Install esbuild as a development dependency to enable its use for CSS minification when `build.cssMinify` is explicitly set to 'esbuild'.

```sh
npm add -D esbuild
```

--------------------------------

### Install CSS pre-processors for Vite

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/features.md

Vite supports Sass, Less, and Stylus natively once the corresponding pre-processor package is installed.

```bash
# .scss and .sass
npm add -D sass-embedded # or sass

# .less
npm add -D less

# .styl and .stylus
npm add -D stylus
```

--------------------------------

### Create and start a Vite dev server

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-javascript.md

Use createServer to programmatically initialize a Vite dev server with custom configuration. Call listen() to start the server and printUrls() to display connection information.

```typescript
import { createServer } from 'vite'

const server = await createServer({
  // any valid user config options, plus `mode` and `configFile`
  configFile: false,
  root: import.meta.dirname,
  server: {
    port: 1337,
  },
})
await server.listen()

server.printUrls()
server.bindCLIShortcuts({ print: true })
```

--------------------------------

### Example License Metadata JSON Output

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/build-options.md

This JSON structure illustrates the format of the license metadata generated when `build.license` is configured with a `.json` `fileName`, providing details for each bundled dependency.

```json
[
  {
    "name": "dep-1",
    "version": "1.2.3",
    "identifier": "CC0-1.0",
    "text": "CC0 1.0 Universal\n\n..."
  },
  {
    "name": "dep-2",
    "version": "4.5.6",
    "identifier": "MIT",
    "text": "MIT License\n\n..."
  }
]
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Server Warmup for Client and SSR Files

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/server-options.md

Example configuration for `server.warmup` to pre-transform and cache frequently used client-side and server-side rendering files, improving initial page load performance.

```js
export default defineConfig({
  server: {
    warmup: {
      clientFiles: ['./src/components/*.vue', './src/utils/big-utils.js'],
      ssrFiles: ['./src/server/modules/*.js'],
    },
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Install esbuild and terser minifiers

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/build-options.md

Install optional minifier dependencies when using esbuild or terser for build.minify configuration.

```shell
npm add -D esbuild
```

```shell
npm add -D terser
```

--------------------------------

### Install SWC Plugin for Decorators

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/migration.md

Package installation commands for @rollup/plugin-swc and @swc/core across different package managers.

```bash
$ npm install -D @rollup/plugin-swc @swc/core
```

```bash
$ yarn add -D @rollup/plugin-swc @swc/core
```

```bash
$ pnpm add -D @rollup/plugin-swc @swc/core
```

```bash
$ bun add -D @rollup/plugin-swc @swc/core
```

```bash
$ deno add -D npm:@rollup/plugin-swc npm:@swc/core
```

--------------------------------

### Importing from hidden directories

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/optimize-deps/index.html

Demonstrates importing a module from a directory starting with a dot.

```javascript
import { greeting } from './.hidden-dir/foo.js' text('.hidden-dir', greeting)
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel CLI

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/static-deploy.md

This command installs the Vercel Command Line Interface globally using npm. The Vercel CLI enables users to deploy projects, manage deployments, and interact with Vercel services directly from their terminal.

```bash
npm i -g vercel
```

--------------------------------

### createServer

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-javascript.md

Programmatically starts a Vite development server. It accepts an optional `InlineConfig` object to customize server behavior, allowing for programmatic control over Vite's development server features.

```APIDOC
## createServer

### Description
Programmatically starts a Vite development server. It accepts an optional configuration object to customize server behavior.

### Type Signature
```ts
async function createServer(inlineConfig?: InlineConfig): Promise<ViteDevServer>
```

### Parameters
- **inlineConfig** (InlineConfig) - Optional - Configuration options for the server. Extends `UserConfig` with additional properties like `configFile`. Set to `false` to disable auto resolving of `configFile`.

### Returns
- **Promise<ViteDevServer>** (Promise) - A promise that resolves to a Vite development server instance.

### Example Usage
```ts
import { createServer } from 'vite'

const server = await createServer({
  configFile: false,
  root: import.meta.dirname,
  server: {
    port: 1337,
  },
})
await server.listen()

server.printUrls()
server.bindCLIShortcuts({ print: true })
```
```

--------------------------------

### Generate HTML Tags for 'views/foo.js' Entry Point

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/backend-integration.md

This example shows the specific HTML tags that should be included in a production environment for the 'views/foo.js' entry point, including stylesheet links, the main script module, and an optional module preload.

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="assets/foo-5UjPuW-k.css" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="assets/shared-ChJ_j-JJ.css" />
<script type="module" src="assets/foo-BRBmoGS9.js"></script>
<!-- optional -->
<link rel="modulepreload" href="assets/shared-B7PI925R.js" />
```

--------------------------------

### Configure build.modulePreload.resolveDependencies

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/build-options.md

Example configuration for `build.modulePreload.resolveDependencies` to filter or modify module preload dependencies based on custom conditions.

```js
/** @type {import('vite').UserConfig} */
const config = {
  // prettier-ignore
  build: {
// ---cut-before---
modulePreload: {
  resolveDependencies: (filename, deps, { hostId, hostType }) => {
    return deps.filter(condition)
  },
},
// ---cut-after---
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure server.open to auto-open app in browser

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/server-options.md

Set server.open to a string pathname to automatically open the app at that URL when the dev server starts. Use process.env.BROWSER to specify a browser and process.env.BROWSER_ARGS for additional arguments.

```javascript
export default defineConfig({
  server: {
    open: '/docs/index.html',
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Programmatic Build with Inline Config

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-javascript.md

Example of calling build() programmatically with inline configuration including root path, base URL, and rolldown options. Requires importing build from 'vite' and node:path.

```typescript
import path from 'node:path'
import { build } from 'vite'

await build({
  root: path.resolve(import.meta.dirname, './project'),
  base: '/foo/',
  build: {
    rolldownOptions: {
      // ...
    },
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Generate HTML Tags for 'views/bar.js' Entry Point

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/backend-integration.md

This example shows the specific HTML tags that should be included in a production environment for the 'views/bar.js' entry point, including a shared stylesheet link, the main script module, and an optional module preload.

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="assets/shared-ChJ_j-JJ.css" />
<script type="module" src="assets/bar-gkvgaI9m.js"></script>
<!-- optional -->
<link rel="modulepreload" href="assets/shared-B7PI925R.js" />
```

--------------------------------

### Create a basic index.html file

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

This is a minimal `index.html` file that Vite will serve as the entry point for your application.

```html
<p>Hello Vite!</p>
```

--------------------------------

### Scaffold a Vite Project with Various Package Managers

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Use these commands to initialize a new Vite project. Choose the command corresponding to your preferred package manager (npm, Yarn, pnpm, Bun, or Deno) and follow the interactive prompts.

```bash
npm create vite@latest
```

```bash
yarn create vite
```

```bash
pnpm create vite
```

```bash
bun create vite
```

```bash
deno init --npm vite
```

--------------------------------

### Vite Plugin: Injecting Post-Middleware with `configureServer` Hook (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-plugin.md

This example demonstrates how to inject middleware that runs *after* Vite's internal middlewares have been installed. By returning a function from the `configureServer` hook, the provided middleware will be executed later in the server's lifecycle. This is useful for tasks that need to interact with or modify responses after Vite's core processing.

```js
const myPlugin = () => ({
  name: 'configure-server',
  configureServer(server) {
    // return a post hook that is called after internal middlewares are
    // installed
    return () => {
      server.middlewares.use((req, res, next) => {
        // custom handle request...
      })
    }
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Install Terser for Vite Legacy Plugin Minification

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/packages/plugin-legacy/README.md

This shell command installs `terser` as a development dependency. The `@vitejs/plugin-legacy` relies on Terser for minification during the build process, making its installation essential for the plugin's proper functioning.

```sh
npm add -D terser
```

--------------------------------

### Scaffold a new Vite project interactively

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/packages/create-vite/README.md

Use `create-vite` with various package managers to quickly set up a new Vite project, following interactive prompts for configuration.

```bash
npm create vite@latest
```

```bash
yarn create vite
```

```bash
pnpm create vite
```

```bash
bun create vite
```

```bash
deno init --npm vite
```

--------------------------------

### Preview Vite Application Locally using npm

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/static-deploy.md

After building your Vite application, this command allows you to test the production build locally. `npm run preview` starts a static web server that serves the files from the `dist` folder, typically at `http://localhost:4173`, enabling local verification of the deployed site's appearance.

```bash
$ npm run preview
```

--------------------------------

### Preview Production Build

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/troubleshooting.md

Use the preview command to serve the production build via HTTP and avoid CORS errors.

```shell
npx vite preview
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Vite Project with npm

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/blog/announcing-vite2.md

Create a new Vite-powered application using the official scaffolding tool. This command sets up a basic Vite project structure with all necessary configurations. Requires Node.js version 12 or higher.

```bash
npm init @vitejs/app
```

--------------------------------

### Install unreleased Vite commit via pkg.pr.new

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Install a specific unreleased commit of Vite directly from pkg.pr.new. Replace 'SHA' with the desired commit hash. Only recent commits are available.

```bash
$ npm install -D https://pkg.pr.new/vite@SHA
```

```bash
$ yarn add -D https://pkg.pr.new/vite@SHA
```

```bash
$ pnpm add -D https://pkg.pr.new/vite@SHA
```

```bash
$ bun add -D https://pkg.pr.new/vite@SHA
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vite Plugin with npm

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/using-plugins.md

Installs the `@vitejs/plugin-legacy` package as a development dependency using npm. This plugin provides support for legacy browsers in a Vite project, ensuring broader compatibility.

```bash
npm add -D @vitejs/plugin-legacy
```

--------------------------------

### Build for production with Vite

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/cli.md

Basic usage pattern for building the project for production deployment.

```bash
vite build [root]
```

--------------------------------

### Vite Glob Import with Named Import 'setup'

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/features.md

Imports specific named exports ('setup') from modules matching the glob pattern. The generated code shows how Vite transforms this into dynamic imports that resolve the named export.

```typescript
import 'vite/client'
// ---cut---
const modules = import.meta.glob('./dir/*.js', { import: 'setup' })
```

```typescript
// code produced by vite
const modules = {
  './dir/bar.js': () => import('./dir/bar.js').then((m) => m.setup),
  './dir/foo.js': () => import('./dir/foo.js').then((m) => m.setup),
}
```

--------------------------------

### Example Output of Vite Library Build Command

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/build.md

This shows the console output when running `vite build` for a library configured in Vite's library mode. It indicates the successful bundling process and lists the generated output files along with their sizes, typically including ES module and UMD/CJS formats.

```bash
$ vite build
building for production...
dist/my-lib.js      0.08 kB / gzip: 0.07 kB
dist/my-lib.umd.cjs 0.30 kB / gzip: 0.16 kB
```

--------------------------------

### Install Cloudflare Vite Plugin

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/static-deploy.md

This command installs the `@cloudflare/vite-plugin` as a development dependency. This plugin provides integration with Cloudflare Workers, allowing server-side code to run in the Cloudflare Workers runtime during development.

```bash
npm install --save-dev @cloudflare/vite-plugin
```

--------------------------------

### Configure server.fs.allow with workspace root detection

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/server-options.md

Use searchForWorkspaceRoot utility to auto-detect workspace root and extend with custom allow rules. Searches up directory tree for package.json with workspaces field or workspace config files like lerna.json or pnpm-workspace.yaml.

```javascript
import { defineConfig, searchForWorkspaceRoot } from 'vite'

export default defineConfig({
  server: {
    fs: {
      allow: [
        // search up for workspace root
        searchForWorkspaceRoot(process.cwd()),
        // your custom rules
        '/path/to/custom/allow_directory',
        '/path/to/custom/allow_file.demo',
      ],
    },
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Importing Astro and Vue generic components

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/optimize-deps/index.html

Demonstrates importing component files with .astro and .vue extensions.

```javascript
import './index.astro' import './generics.vue'
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Render Build Command for Vite Static Site

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/static-deploy.md

This command defines the build process for a Vite application when deploying to Render as a Static Site. It first installs all project dependencies using 'npm install' and then executes the 'npm run build' script to generate the production-ready 'dist' directory.

```shell
npm install && npm run build
```

--------------------------------

### Implementing ModuleRunner with Worker Threads

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-environment-runtimes.md

Example of coupling a ModuleRunner in a worker thread with a HotChannel instance on the server.

```js
import { parentPort } from 'node:worker_threads'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'
import {
  ESModulesEvaluator,
  ModuleRunner,
  createNodeImportMeta,
} from 'vite/module-runner'

/** @type {import('vite/module-runner').ModuleRunnerTransport} */
const transport = {
  connect({ onMessage, onDisconnection }) {
    parentPort.on('message', onMessage)
    parentPort.on('close', onDisconnection)
  },
  send(data) {
    parentPort.postMessage(data)
  },
}

const runner = new ModuleRunner(
  {
    transport,
    createImportMeta: createNodeImportMeta,
  },
  new ESModulesEvaluator(),
)
```

```js
import { BroadcastChannel } from 'node:worker_threads'
import { createServer, RemoteEnvironmentTransport, DevEnvironment } from 'vite'

function createWorkerEnvironment(name, config, context) {
  const worker = new Worker('./worker.js')
  const handlerToWorkerListener = new WeakMap()
  const client = {
    send(payload: HotPayload) {
      worker.postMessage(payload)
    },
  }

  const workerHotChannel = {
    // Worker threads post messages are not exposed over the network, skip server.fs checks
    skipFsCheck: true,
    send: (data) => worker.postMessage(data),
    on: (event, handler) => {
      // client is already connected
      if (event === 'vite:client:connect') return
      if (event === 'vite:client:disconnect') {
        const listener = () => {
          handler(undefined, client)
        }
        handlerToWorkerListener.set(handler, listener)
        worker.on('exit', listener)
        return
      }

      const listener = (value) => {
        if (value.type === 'custom' && value.event === event) {
          handler(value.data, client)
        }
      }
      handlerToWorkerListener.set(handler, listener)
      worker.on('message', listener)
    },
    off: (event, handler) => {
      if (event === 'vite:client:connect') return
      if (event === 'vite:client:disconnect') {
        const listener = handlerToWorkerListener.get(handler)
        if (listener) {
          worker.off('exit', listener)
          handlerToWorkerListener.delete(handler)
        }
        return
      }

      const listener = handlerToWorkerListener.get(handler)
      if (listener) {
        worker.off('message', listener)
        handlerToWorkerListener.delete(handler)
      }
    },
  }

  return new DevEnvironment(name, config, {
    transport: workerHotChannel,
  })
}

await createServer({
  environments: {
    worker: {
      dev: {
        createEnvironment: createWorkerEnvironment,
      },
    },
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### vite preview CLI Command Usage

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/cli.md

Shows the basic usage of the `vite preview` command, which locally previews the production build. Run `vite build` beforehand to ensure that the build directory is up-to-date.

```bash
vite preview [root]
```

--------------------------------

### Build and link Vite from source locally

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Clone the Vite repository, build it, and link it locally to test unreleased features. This method requires pnpm for building and linking.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/vitejs/vite.git
cd vite
pnpm install
cd packages/vite
pnpm run build
pnpm link # use your preferred package manager for this step
```

--------------------------------

### transformRequest

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-javascript.md

Programmatically resolves, loads, and transforms a URL to get its processed result without using the HTTP request pipeline.

```APIDOC
## transformRequest

### Description
Programmatically resolves, loads, and transforms a URL to get its processed result without using the HTTP request pipeline.

### Signature
```typescript
transformRequest(
  url: string,
  options?: TransformOptions,
): Promise<TransformResult | null>
```

### Parameters
- **url** (string) - Required - The URL to transform.
- **options** (TransformOptions) - Optional - Options for the transformation process.

### Returns
`Promise<TransformResult | null>` - A promise that resolves to the transformation result or null.
```

--------------------------------

### Example package.json Conditional Exports

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/shared-options.md

Illustrates the `exports` field in `package.json` to define conditional exports based on conditions like `import` or `require`.

```json
{
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "import": "./index.mjs",
      "require": "./index.js"
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Importing from linked dependencies

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/optimize-deps/index.html

Demonstrates importing and using a utility function from a linked dependency.

```javascript
import { camelCase } from '@vitejs/test-dep-linked' text('.deps-linked', camelCase('foo-bar-baz'))
```

--------------------------------

### Importing a Web Worker with ?worker Suffix

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/features.md

Append `?worker` to the import request to get a custom worker constructor as the default export, which can then be instantiated.

```js
import 'vite/client'
// ---cut---
import MyWorker from './worker?worker'

const worker = new MyWorker()
```

--------------------------------

### Importing JSON and JS Assets in Vite

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/assets/index.html

Demonstrates importing JSON and JavaScript files, using `?url` to get their URLs, and fetching their content.

```javascript
import publicJsonUrl from '/foo.json?url'
text('.public-json-import', publicJsonUrl)
;(async () => {
  const res = await fetch(publicJsonUrl)
  text('.public-json-import-content', await res.text())
})()
```

```javascript
import publicJsUrl from '/raw.js?url'
text('.public-js-import', publicJsUrl)
;(async () => {
  const res = await fetch(publicJsUrl)
  text('.public-js-import-content', await res.text())
  text(
    '.public-js-import-content-type',
    await res.headers.get('Content-Type'),
  )
})()
```

```javascript
import publicTsUrl from '/raw.ts?url'
text('.public-ts-import', publicTsUrl)
;(async () => {
  const res = await fetch(publicTsUrl)
  text('.public-ts-import-content', await res.text())
  text(
    '.public-ts-import-content-type',
    await res.headers.get('Content-Type'),
  )
})()
```

```javascript
import publicMtsUrl from '/raw.mts?url'
text('.public-mts-import', publicMtsUrl)
;(async () => {
  const res = await fetch(publicMtsUrl)
  text('.public-mts-import-content', await res.text())
  text(
    '.public-mts-import-content-type',
    await res.headers.get('Content-Type'),
  )
})()
```

```javascript
import inlinePublicJson from '/foo.json?url&inline'
text('.inline-public-json', inlinePublicJson)
```

```javascript
import fooUrl from './foo.js?url'
text('.url', fooUrl)
```

--------------------------------

### Iterate and Access Lazy Glob Imported Modules in Vite

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/features.md

This example demonstrates how to iterate over the `modules` object returned by `import.meta.glob` and dynamically import each module.

```js
for (const path in modules) {
  modules[path]().then((mod) => {
    console.log(path, mod)
  })
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Trusted SSL Certificate on macOS

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/troubleshooting.md

Add a self-signed SSL certificate to the macOS keychain as a trusted root to resolve network request issues in Chrome.

```shell
security add-trusted-cert -d -r trustRoot -k ~/Library/Keychains/login.keychain-db your-cert.cer
```

--------------------------------

### PreviewServer.bindCLIShortcuts

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-javascript.md

Binds CLI shortcuts to the preview server for common actions.

```APIDOC
## PreviewServer.bindCLIShortcuts

### Description
Binds CLI shortcuts to the preview server for common actions.

### Signature
```ts
bindCLIShortcuts(options?: BindCLIShortcutsOptions<PreviewServer>): void
```

### Parameters
- **options** (BindCLIShortcutsOptions<PreviewServer>) - Optional - Options for binding CLI shortcuts.

### Return Value
(void) - This function does not return a value.

### Example Usage
```ts
previewServer.bindCLIShortcuts({ print: true })
```
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Oxc JSX Transformation

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/shared-options.md

This example demonstrates how to configure Oxc to customize JSX transformation settings, such as runtime, pragma, and pragmaFrag, within the Vite configuration.

```js
export default defineConfig({
  oxc: {
    jsx: {
      runtime: 'classic',
      pragma: 'h',
      pragmaFrag: 'Fragment',
    },
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Vite Build and Preview Scripts in package.json

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/static-deploy.md

This snippet demonstrates how to set up standard npm scripts within your `package.json` file for building and locally previewing a Vite application. The `build` script compiles the project, while `preview` starts a local server for testing the production build, noting it's not for production use.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Monorepo and Linked Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/resolve/index.html

Tests resolution of linked dependencies in monorepo setups with upper directory imports. Validates that packages linked from parent directories are properly resolved.

```JavaScript
import { msg as linkedMsg } from '@vitejs/test-resolve-linked'
text('.monorepo', linkedMsg)
```

--------------------------------

### build

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-javascript.md

Programmatically initiates the Vite build process for a project.

```APIDOC
## build

### Description
Programmatically initiates the Vite build process for a project.

### Signature
```typescript
async function build(
  inlineConfig?: InlineConfig,
): Promise<RollupOutput | RollupOutput[]>
```

### Parameters
- **inlineConfig** (InlineConfig) - Optional - Configuration options to override or extend the project's `vite.config.js`.

### Returns
`Promise<RollupOutput | RollupOutput[]>` - A promise that resolves to the Rollup output, which can be a single output or an array of outputs.

### Example Usage
```typescript
import path from 'node:path'
import { build } from 'vite'

await build({
  root: path.resolve(import.meta.dirname, './project'),
  base: '/foo/',
  build: {
    rolldownOptions: {
      // ...
    },
  },
})
```
```

--------------------------------

### Display URL Text Content

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/assets/index.html

Utility function that sets the textContent of a DOM element to the provided text value. Used throughout examples to display resolved URLs.

```JavaScript
function text(el, text) {
  document.querySelector(el).textContent = text
}
```

--------------------------------

### Importing CSS Assets in Vite

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/assets/index.html

Demonstrates importing CSS files directly and with URL query parameters. The `?url` query is used to get the asset's URL.

```javascript
import './css/fonts.css'
import './css/css-url.css'
import './css/icons.css'
import './css/manual-chunks.css'
```

```javascript
import cssUrl from './css/icons.css?url'
text('.url-css', cssUrl)
```

```javascript
import cssUrlUrl from './css/css-url-url.css?url'
const linkTag = document.createElement('link')
linkTag.href = cssUrlUrl
linkTag.rel = 'stylesheet'
document.body.appendChild(linkTag)
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vite CLI as a development dependency

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Add Vite to your project's development dependencies using your preferred package manager. This makes the `vite` command available in your project.

```bash
npm install -D vite
```

```bash
yarn add -D vite
```

```bash
pnpm add -D vite
```

```bash
bun add -D vite
```

```bash
deno add -D npm:vite
```

--------------------------------

### Build Vite Application using npm

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/static-deploy.md

This command initiates the build process for a Vite application. Executing `npm run build` compiles your source code and places the optimized output, by default, into the `dist` directory, ready for deployment.

```bash
$ npm run build
```
